---
title: Add a site language
description: How to add a new site language to your LightNet media site.
---

import { Aside, Steps } from "@astrojs/starlight/components";

Follow these steps to add a new site language to your LightNet project:

<Steps>
1. **Identify the language code**: Find the correct [BCP-47 language code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IETF_language_tag) for your language, for example `en` for English or `de` for German.
   Tools like [r12a Language Subtag Lookup](https://r12a.github.io/app-subtags/) can help you find the right code.
1. **Add the language to your config**: Add the new language to the `languages` array in `astro.config.mjs`.
    ```js title="astro.config.mjs" ins={5-11}
    export default defineConfig({
        integrations: [
            lightnet({
                languages: [
                    {
                        code: "de",
                        label: {
                            en: "German",
                            de: "Deutsch"
                        },
                        isSiteLanguage: true
                    },
                ],
            }),
        ],
    })
    ```
1. **Create a translations file**: Add a file in `src/translations` named after your language code, for example `src/translations/de.yml`.
1. **Check built-in LightNet translations**: Inspect LightNet's [built-in translations on GitHub](https://github.com/LightNetDev/LightNet/tree/main/packages/lightnet/src/i18n/translations) and look for a file named `[your-language-code].yml`.
   - If no such file exists, LightNet does not yet provide built-in translations for that language.
   - If the file exists, check the [translation status](https://github.com/LightNetDev/LightNet/blob/main/packages/lightnet/src/i18n/translations/TRANSLATION-STATUS.md) to see whether any keys are still missing.
1. **Add missing built-in translations to your project**: If your language is not built in yet, or if the built-in translation file is incomplete, copy the missing keys from the [English translation file](https://github.com/LightNetDev/LightNet/blob/main/packages/lightnet/src/i18n/translations/en.yml) into your project translation file and translate those values.
1. **Add your custom translation keys**: If your project already uses custom translation keys in other locale files, add translated values for those keys to the new file as well.
1. **Set the default language if needed**: Your site must have exactly one default site language.
   If the new language should become the default, add `isDefaultSiteLanguage: true` to that entry and remove `isDefaultSiteLanguage` from the current default language.
1. **Validate the new locale**: Run `npm run dev` and check that the new language appears in the language selector.
   Switch to the new locale and verify that built-in strings, your custom translations, and language labels display correctly.
1. **Contribute upstream**: If you translated missing LightNet built-in strings, consider sharing them through this [translations update form](https://github.com/LightNetDev/LightNet/issues/new?template=---03-translations-update.yml).
</Steps>

<Aside type="tip">
LightNet resolves translations from both built-in locale files and your project's `src/translations` files.
Project translations take precedence, so you only need to add the keys that are missing or that you want to override.
</Aside>
